summary.intent: Add a 'QE' approval group to a GitLab MR using CLI tools, then automate MR approval rule management by adding an mr_approvers field to ocp-build-data group.yml and updating the release_from_fbc pipeline in art-tools to configure approval rules when creating shipment MRs.
summary.outcome: Successfully added and managed approval rules on MR !453 using curl commands. Implemented full automation: added mr_approvers schema support, created GitLab client method for approval rule management, integrated approval rule setup into release_from_fbc pipeline, configured logging-6.5 group.yml, and fixed two validation bugs (dry-run placeholder URL issue and YAML type validation).

repo: GitLab project-level approval rules can't be fully deleted at MR level - DELETE only removes overrides, rules reappear from project inheritance
ocp-build-data has multiple logging-* branches requiring separate configurations
art-tools uses python-gitlab library for GitLab API, not raw HTTP
Group configs are read via 'doozer config:read-group' command pattern
JSON schema for group.yml has additionalProperties: false so all new fields must be explicitly added to properties

code.finding: Added set_mr_approval_rules method using python-gitlab library's native approval_rules API (mr.approval_rules.list/create/delete)
During dry-run, mr_url is set to placeholder '.../placeholder/placeholder/-/merge_requests/placeholder' which doesn't exist in GitLab
Calling set_mr_approval_rules during dry-run fails because get_mr_from_url tries to resolve placeholder URL before checking dry_run flag - fixed by checking self.dry_run before API call
stdlib_yaml.safe_load can return non-dict types (string, list, int) - added isinstance check and type validation before returning to callers
New top-level group.yml fields must be added to properties object due to additionalProperties: false constraint

workflow: glab api supports --hostname flag for targeting specific GitLab instances with GITLAB_TOKEN auth
glab api doesn't properly encode array parameters (user_ids[]) - curl with JSON body is more reliable
GitLab API uses URL-encoded project paths (e.g., hybrid-platforms%2Fart%2Focp-shipment-data)
User lookup: GET /users?username=<name>, MR approval rules: POST /projects/:id/merge_requests/:iid/approval_rules
DELETE approval rule returns 204 on success (empty response)
Git workflow: fetch prod, branch from appropriate prod branch, push to origin fork, PR to prod

friction: glab api -f 'user_ids[]=15399' array encoding failed, required fallback to curl with JSON body
GitLab DELETE for project-inherited approval rules returns 204 success but rules reappear due to project-level inheritance - confusing behavior
set_mr_approval_rules tried to resolve placeholder MR URL during dry-run before checking dry_run flag
stdlib_yaml.safe_load type assumption caused potential runtime errors with non-dict YAML content
open_items: Apply mr_approvers field to other logging-* branches beyond logging-6.5
Test pipeline changes with actual release_from_fbc run for end-to-end validation
Create and merge PRs to prod/main (art-tools) and prod/logging-6.5 (ocp-build-data)
Run unit tests for changed files (mentioned in plan but not executed in transcript)
